title: solidago canadensis subsp. altissima
intro: Solidago canadensis subsp. altissima, also known as tall goldenrod, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Solidago canadensis subsp. altissima have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
